validation_rows.requirement: P3-SPEC-CLOSURE review is approved and subject-bound
schema-bound element artifacts use current required fields exactly
all element artifacts declare the standard envelope, records payload, and validator refs
explicit inventory keeps natural language as semantic authority and inventory as index and verification skeleton
every eligible closure node, clickable, message, failure recovery, async, overlay, and handoff object maps to canonical element or blocked gap

canonical projection includes only adopt-as-is, adopt-normalized, eligible deductive derived rows, and Human Gate resolved approved expansion rows; all clarification, conflict, gap-routed, open-gap, structural-review, blocked, and ineligible rows are excluded
validation_rows.validator: adoption, reasoning, and canonical projection validators

validation_rows.requirement: deductive completion can make canonical elements only when it does not add product capability; induction remains blocked until Human Gate approval
structural completion review routes missing child pages or ambiguous lower-level surfaces before adoption
candidate scope stays inside P3-SPEC-ELEMENTS and does not create locks or implementation code

minimum_test_obligations: inventory row that bundles page, input, and CTA is rejected as non-atomic
explicit inventory row without adoption decision is rejected
eligible closure clickable without canonical element or blocked gap is rejected
canonical derived element with INDUCTIVE_AUXILIARY is rejected without Human Gate approval
deductive element that adds product capability is routed to product_expansion_gaps and blocked downstream
page-only source that implies missing child pages requires structural_completion_review before adoption
input obligation without acquisition_path or gap_ref is rejected
downstream pattern or layout reference to unknown element id is rejected
natural language conflict with inventory row blocks the row until corrected or reviewed
REQUEST_CLARIFICATION, REJECT_CONFLICT, ROUTE_PRODUCT_GAP, open-gap, structural-review, and blocked derived rows are excluded from canonical projection
